title: malcolmia grandiflora var. glabrescens
intro: Malcolmia grandiflora var. glabrescens is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the white stock or white stock mustard. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant qualities, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, skin irritations, and digestive problems. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce fever, soothe coughs, and promote wound healing. Malcolmia grandiflora var. glabrescens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
